beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home. Water the houseplants and check on them.
beats.reasoning: Sunday morning wake-up; Harper's energy was low yesterday (45/100) after a full teaching day and evening out, so a gentle start with plant care and coffee feels restorative and on-brand.

beats.activity: Walk to a nearby café for a leisurely brunch.
beats.reasoning: Sunday mood calls for lingering over food; Café Solstice is a known haunt and walkable from Belltown via Pike Place area, fitting Harper's spontaneous, neighborhood-oriented style.

beats.activity: Brunch with coffee, reading or journaling, watching the Capitol Hill crowd wake up.
beats.reasoning: Low-energy recovery day after yesterday's drain; Harper values quiet time and is spontaneous enough to linger over a meal without a packed schedule.

beats.activity: Walk to the pottery studio to throw some clay.
beats.reasoning: Harper is already in Capitol Hill and yesterday mentioned the studio being a tactile reset; sunny weather and low energy make this a natural pull for a Sunday creative session.

beats.activity: Throw pottery, focus on centering and a few small bowls or vessels.
beats.reasoning: Harper's hands-on creative outlet; Sunday afternoon is ideal for unhurried studio time, especially after low energy yesterday. The sunny weather outside makes the cool studio appealing.

beats.activity: Grab a quick lunch at a nearby deli or market.
beats.reasoning: Still in Capitol Hill; quick refuel before heading toward Lake Union to take advantage of the sunny afternoon and light recreational energy.

beats.activity: Walk or bike to Gas Works Park to watch the afternoon boat traffic on Lake Union.
beats.reasoning: Sunny Sunday, Lake Union is busy with recreational traffic, and Harper is spontaneous and competitive—watching others paddle/boat while enjoying the view fits the mood and the world event.

beats.activity: Sit at Gas Works Park, take film photography shots of boats and lake light, people-watch, relax.
beats.reasoning: Harper does film photography; the afternoon light and recreational boat traffic provide perfect subjects. Competitive edge means enjoying watching others' activity; low energy makes passive observation ideal.
Evening settling in; Harper heads home as the day cools, allowing time to decompress before evening leisure.

beats.activity: Cook a simple dinner, eat at home, play guitar or listen to music.
beats.reasoning: Sunday evening wind-down; Harper cooks and spends time with music or guitar, staying home after an active but low-key day. Restorative and on-brand.

beats.activity: Relax at home—read, watch a film, tend to houseplants, light journaling, wind down.
beats.reasoning: Evening leisure to close out Sunday; Harper values quiet time and is energetically low, so staying home and doing low-key creative or reflective activities fits the day's trajectory.
Eight hours of sleep to recover from yesterday's drain and prepare for the week ahead. Sleep beat spans overnight to 6:30 AM Monday as required.

diary: Woke up to perfect weather and just... let the day unfold. Watered the fiddle leaf fig—it's looking healthier lately—then wandered over to Café Solstice for brunch. Something about Sunday mornings there, watching Capitol Hill come alive with coffee in hand, feels sacred. Spent the morning at the pottery studio throwing bowls, that meditative spin on the wheel. My hands remembered what they needed to do even when my mind wasn't fully there. Grabbed lunch, then biked out to Gas Works Park with my film camera. The lake was alive with boats, all that summer energy. I shot maybe two rolls, mostly just sat on the grass watching light bounce off the water and people move through their weekends. By evening I was home cooking something simple, playing guitar softly, tending to my plants again. The kind of Sunday that doesn't ask much of you—just presence.
thought_process: The calm weather shaped everything today. No urgency, no rain to work around. I chose the pottery studio because my body needed that tactile focus, and brunch gave me space to sit without guilt. The decision to bike to Gas Works instead of staying home felt right when I thought about how alive the lake would be—I needed to be around that energy, even just observing it. My mood stayed gentle throughout, never spiking, never dipping. The film camera choice felt important too; it slows me down, makes me look harder. There's something about the consistency of my Sunday routine—café, clay, lake, home—that grounds me. Teaching guitar all week means I need these unstructured hours to remember why I make music in the first place.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
